ps140: 4, Podoben “As one valiant among the martyrs…”

Like winged eagles you passed over all the earth, sowing precious doctrines,* uprooting the tares of falsehood by grace,* and producing much grain as fruit, O most lauded ones;* and the noetic granaries hold it forever, laid up in abundance* for the immortal Husbandman.

Let us bless Herodion, Agabus and Asyncretus,* Rufus and the divine Phlegontus,* the branches of a fruitful vine who pour forth upon us the sweetness of salvation,* which with divine faith make glad the thirsting hearts* of those who celebrate their godly memory.

Higher than heaven in your thought,* you declared the glory of our God* who became incarnate of His own will, O you apostles, beholders of God,* instructors of the pious, foundations of the Churches,* unshakable pillars, refuges of piety,* servants of ineffable mysteries, leaders of our souls into light.
theotokion: O most pure one, who in your womb contained the infinite God* who, in His love for mankind, became man,* received our matter from you, and manifestly deified it:* disdain me not who am now sorrowful,* but quickly take pity and free me from diverse enemies* and the malice of the evil one.
stavrotheotokion: As she beheld You nailed to the Cross, O Lord,* the ewe-lamb, Your Mother, marvelled and cried out:* What is this vision which I see, O my Son most desired?* How have the iniquitous and unbelieving assembly recompensed You,* which enjoyed Your many miracles?* Yet glory, O Master, to Your ineffable condescension!
